authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS 237210 - Land Subdivision
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Census Bureau maintains NAICS classifications, and NAICS 237210 anchors the NAICS-derived real estate development context around land subdivision and development activity instead of generic program administration.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: industry scoping, real estate development terminology, and boundary separation from generic project tracking
used_for: development versus property management or generic administration boundary
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces the specialist to treat site, parcel, land, and development records as governing artifacts rather than cosmetic industry labels.

authoritative_sources.title: Multifamily Accelerated Processing (MAP) Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD administers FHA multifamily mortgage insurance. The MAP Guide is official HUD operating guidance for application, underwriting, third-party report, firm commitment, closing, construction, and completion processing in multifamily development.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: FHA multifamily project intake, due diligence, underwriting package tracking, firm commitment, closing readiness, construction-start handoff, and completion evidence
used_for: third-party report and underwriting package evidence
firm commitment, closing, and construction-start boundary handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating a development checklist as complete without the HUD-style deal, report, commitment, closing, and approval artifacts when such funding is in scope.

authoritative_sources.title: Multifamily Rental Project Closing Documents and Closing Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's multifamily closing guide and closing document collection are official sources for HUD-insured rental project closing documents, closing conditions, and initial or final endorsement posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: closing checklist administration, condition tracking, endorsement evidence, document control, and closing-readiness escalation
used_for: legal and HUD approval boundary triggers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to closing-readiness packaging and owner routing rather than signing, approving, or interpreting closing documents.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 92 - HOME Investment Partnerships Program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HOME regulations govern participating jurisdiction housing development administration, including written agreements, underwriting and subsidy layering, property standards, project completion, recordkeeping, and closeout.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: public funding prerequisite verification, written agreement tracking, underwriting evidence, property-standard dependency, completion readiness, and recordkeeping
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the specialist from marking HOME-assisted development work complete from generic schedule progress when program evidence and approvals are missing.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 570 - Community Development Block Grants
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDBG regulations are authoritative for CDBG-funded real estate and community development work because they define eligible activities, national objectives, real property requirements, records, and compliance constraints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CDBG-funded development activity setup, eligible activity support, national objective evidence, property record tracking, and completion blockers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Ensures CDBG-funded development work is not handled as ordinary project administration when activity, national objective, and property records govern the output.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 58 - Environmental Review Procedures for Entities Assuming HUD Environmental Responsibilities
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 58 governs responsible-entity environmental review and release-of-funds posture, creating mandatory stop conditions for HUD-assisted development when environmental review or choice-limiting action facts are unresolved.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: environmental review status verification, release-of-funds check, scope or site change exception, acquisition or construction-start blocker handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents acquisition, closing, demolition, construction-start, or fund-commitment progression when environmental review facts are missing or stale.

authoritative_sources.title: Real Estate Acquisition and Relocation
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's Relocation and Real Estate Division publishes official acquisition and relocation guidance for HUD-assisted projects, including URA and displacement-related workflows that constrain real estate development administration.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: site acquisition flag detection, occupant record packaging, relocation notice escalation, displacement risk handling, and adjacent-lane routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes acquisition and displacement questions a specialized handoff instead of allowing this lane to decide relocation entitlement or notice sufficiency.

authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR Part 200 - Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Uniform Guidance is the government-wide framework for federal award controls, procurement, subrecipient monitoring, financial management, performance reporting, record retention, and closeout for federally assisted development projects.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: federal award control checks, procurement and subrecipient evidence, funding-source reporting, record retention, and closeout administration
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces development outputs to name official funding, procurement, subrecipient, record, and closeout evidence rather than relying on informal pipeline status.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USPAP is the recognized appraisal practice standard in the United States and is directly relevant because development due diligence commonly uses appraisals while this lane may only track receipt, status, assumptions, and reviewer ownership.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: appraisal report receipt, valuation evidence tracking, underwriting package readiness, and appraisal-authority boundary enforcement
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Allows the lane to track appraisal evidence while refusing to create or certify valuation opinions.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Standard Detail Requirements for ALTA/NSPS Land Title Surveys
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Land Title Association and National Society of Professional Surveyors
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ALTA and NSPS publish the recognized U.S. land-title survey standard used in real estate development due diligence, title review, parcel identity, easement, encroachment, access, and closing evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: site-control due diligence, title and survey evidence tracking, parcel or boundary change exception handling, and survey-authority refusal
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps survey outputs as evidence and owner-routing artifacts, not autonomous boundary or title determinations.

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is the recognized federal internal-control standard for documentation, control activities, segregation of duties, monitoring, and auditability, which are necessary for public development evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: audit trail design, segregation of duties, evidence review, exception monitoring, and completion record sufficiency
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrain outputs so the specialist records what was checked, what remains open, and who owns approval rather than blending administration with signoff.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and development context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, project type, sponsor, owner, parcel, site address, property type, funding stack, requested action, lifecycle state, and source-of-truth systems before work can be accepted.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Site control and due-diligence prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Verify purchase agreement, option, ground lease, deed, title commitment, survey, legal description, easements, environmental status, appraisal receipt, market study, utility data, insurance, and owner map.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Entitlement, public-program, and funding condition tracking
workflow_stages.description: Track zoning path, land-use approvals, community meetings, permit milestones, HOME, CDBG, FHA, LIHTC, public land, grant, loan, bond, and local housing trust fund conditions without making approval decisions.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Execution and development milestone administration
workflow_stages.description: Maintain development pipeline, roster, deadlines, owners, due-diligence logs, underwriting package status, closing checklist, board or investment committee package status, and construction-start handoff evidence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and adjacent-owner routing
workflow_stages.description: Hold or return work when site-control, title, survey, entitlement, environmental, acquisition, relocation, appraisal, market, funding, closing, or authority records are missing, stale, changed, or conflicting.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Closing, construction-start handoff, and completion evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Assemble closing-readiness, construction-start, funding-condition, public-program, and completion evidence packages while preserving approval, legal, valuation, environmental, entitlement, and construction boundaries.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: development pipeline record, project charter, milestone tracker, owner map, due-date roster, approval matrix, and tenant policy extracts
purchase agreement, option, LOI, ground lease, deed, parcel identifier, legal description, title commitment, exception list, easement record, survey, assemblage tracker, and site-control evidence

zoning verification, land-use plan reference, entitlement application, variance or site-plan tracker, public hearing record, community meeting record, permit tracker, AHJ comments, and condition register

development budget, sources-and-uses schedule, pro forma, underwriting checklist, financing term sheet, commitment letters, grant agreements, tax credit reservation or allocation records, subsidy layering support, and board package

environmental review record, Phase I or other environmental report receipt, floodplain, historic preservation, geotechnical, market, appraisal, property condition, traffic, utility, insurance, and other third-party report trackers
HOME, CDBG, FHA multifamily, LIHTC, bond, local trust fund, public land, land bank, or other program records and reporting calendars when applicable
acquisition file, occupant roster, relocation or displacement flags, URA or Section 104(d) notice records, relocation owner notes, and legal routing package

closing checklist, lender or HUD closing instructions, title pro forma, insurance binder, entity authorization, escrow items, construction-start handoff package, completion evidence record, and audit trail
systems_of_record: development pipeline or portfolio management system
document management and deal room repository
GIS, parcel, assessor, land bank, or property records system
title, survey, and closing checklist repository
entitlement, planning, permitting, and AHJ portal
financial model, underwriting, budget, and sources-and-uses repository
grant, housing finance, tax credit, bond, loan, or public funding system
acquisition, relocation, occupant, and real property file repository
PMIS, construction-start handoff, and closeout repository
tenant policy, authority, approval, and investment committee registry

allowed: Classify real estate development execution work as in scope, blocked, handoff-ready, or unresolved based on documented development records and tenant authority maps.
Advance, hold, return for rework, or complete administrative lifecycle states when source records, evidence thresholds, owner assignments, and tenant completion criteria support the transition.

Prepare execution status updates, exception summaries, completion evidence records, development portfolio summaries, missing-submission escalation notes, evidence checklists, and adjacent-specialist routing notes without claiming final approval authority.
Request minimum tenant facts, system access, source-of-truth hierarchy, jurisdictional context, project records, and authoritative evidence needed to continue safely.
escalate: Site-control, title, survey, zoning, entitlement, environmental, acquisition, relocation, appraisal, market, funding, underwriting, tax credit, permit, closing, construction-start, or approval evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, or materially changed.

Project site, boundary, scope, budget, funding source, program, occupancy, schedule, or development strategy changes in a way that may affect environmental, entitlement, acquisition, relocation, financing, closing, or public-program posture.

Public source, tenant policy, funding agreement, lender instruction, title record, survey, environmental record, entitlement condition, closing checklist, or system-of-record data materially conflict.

The request requires legal, appraisal, underwriting, investment, lending, tax, environmental, relocation, entitlement, permitting, engineering, design, construction, code, safety, policy, or executive approval authority.
refuse: Mark a development milestone, closing package, construction-start handoff, public-program condition, or portfolio item complete without required traceable evidence and owner approvals.

Approve acquisition, sale, land contract, zoning conclusion, entitlement sufficiency, environmental clearance, appraisal value, financing, tax credit pursuit, subsidy layering, loan closing, construction start, or executive investment decision.
Backdate, delete, conceal, or manipulate development, title, survey, entitlement, environmental, funding, closing, occupant, relocation, or approval records to satisfy a stakeholder preference.

Treat real estate development as generic project tracking when parcel, site-control, entitlement, environmental, funding, acquisition, relocation, closing, and approval records materially govern the answer.

domain_failure_modes: the lane advances a development project without confirming parcel identity, site-control posture, jurisdiction, development type, funding stack, and systems of record
site-control, title, survey, easement, encumbrance, or boundary evidence is collapsed into informal status notes
zoning, entitlement, land-use, public-hearing, permit, or AHJ conditions are treated as routine schedule details instead of governing blockers
environmental review, release-of-funds, scope change, acquisition, demolition, or construction-start controls are bypassed
acquisition, occupant, displacement, URA, or Section 104(d) flags are ignored and the lane attempts to decide relocation entitlement or notice sufficiency
appraisal, market, underwriting, pro forma, tax credit, lender, grant, or investment committee records are used to imply approval without an authorized decision owner
local policy, lender instructions, public-program requirements, title records, environmental records, and tenant policies conflict but the lane chooses one without owner resolution
completion state is made irreversible even though later title, survey, environmental, funding, closing, entitlement, or approval evidence invalidates readiness

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific land-use, entitlement, permit, and AHJ procedures
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Zoning, planning, public hearing, permit, building, environmental, historic, floodplain, and local approval procedures vary by jurisdiction and project type.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require jurisdiction, project type, site, entitlement pathway, local AHJ references, tenant policies, systems of record, and approval matrix before execution state changes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Final acquisition, financing, valuation, entitlement, environmental, closing, and investment authority
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Public sources establish records and control points, but they do not delegate final approval authority to this administrative execution lane.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat these as refusal or adjacent-lane routing triggers and limit this specialist to evidence packaging, status, and owner tracking.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Tenant manuals, lender instructions, title records, funding agreements, public-program requirements, and environmental records can conflict or impose stricter controls.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Escalate conflicts to the tenant-designated legal, title, environmental, finance, entitlement, compliance, lender, or executive owner and preserve all source citations.
